event: View of the apse of the Church of St. Martin-sur-Renelle
symbols: gothic pointed arches — the aspiration of the medieval soul toward heaven
buttressed apse — the structural and spiritual backbone of the church building
description: A detailed etching or ink drawing of the apse end of a Gothic church viewed from the street. The stone building features tall pointed-arch windows with tracery, buttresses, and a steeply angled roofline. The masonry is carefully rendered with individual stones visible. A small projecting chapel or sacristy extends from the lower portion. Clouds appear to the left. The drawing has a precise, architectural quality with fine line work throughout.

_meta.title: the Apse of the Church of St. Martin-sur-renelle
